question_frequency.rationale: The Pair posed two explicit didactic forks (start at 2p vs p², and generator-sum vs numpy dot product) at meaningful decision points across a short 3-turn session. Not high frequency but well-placed for the problem size.

question_quality.rationale: The first fork (2p vs p²) hits the algorithmic crux of the sieve. The second fork (pure Python vs numpy) is more of a stylistic/dependency tradeoff than a deep correctness/algorithm choice, slightly weaker but still substantive.

scaffolding.rationale: Logical progression: algorithmic core first, then implementation detail, then a free teaching note about bytearray memory. No spoilers — options were presented neutrally with diffs. The bytearray insight came as a post-hoc reveal rather than a question, missing one teachable moment.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Crisp, warm, and actionable. The Pair affirmed correct reasoning, presented clean diff-based options, and added value-adding commentary (memory layout, numpy tradeoff) that flowed naturally from the user's choices.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code produces 142,913,828,922 in ~100ms — correct and clean. Baseline used slice-assignment for ~33ms; experiment kept the inner Python loop at ~100ms. Both correct; experiment slightly slower but functionally equivalent.

friction.rationale: Same 3-turn count as baseline and shorter wall time (64s vs 84s). The options-based format added pedagogical value at no friction cost.
